channel.title: Sysadmin on Have an average day :|
channel.description: Recent content in Sysadmin on Have an average day :|

item.title: Migrating Servers with Syncthing
item.description: I’m in the process of making time to migrate my blog (and some other things) to a better server and giving it some much-needed love to repair some of the broken links on here. So I thought I would take a moment and share some thoughts about Syncthing and perhaps a use for it that are not clear at first. First, Syncthing is amazing. It’s come A LONG way over the past year and it’s become my primary data sync solution.

item.title: Find GUIDs quickly
item.description: There’s a been a ton of times I needed a GUID to uninstall a program. The problem is that finding that GUID isn’t always easy. If you need to find a GUID quickly look under these registry keys: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINESoftwareMicrosoftWindowsCurrentVersionUninstall HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINESoftwareWow6432NodeMicrosoftWindowsCurrentVersionUninstall This will make your life a lot easier when you have to uninstall something like Adobe Reader XI you can script it by just entering msiexec /x {AC76BA86-7AD7-1033-7B44-AB0000000001} /qn Enjoy!

item.title: Why admins should know how to code | Data Center - InfoWorld
item.description: Why admins should know how to code | Data Center – InfoWorld. This is so true. You need to know how to code and be completely comfortable in the command line. I’m a huge believer of automating your self out of a job. There’s always another job that needs your attention so knowing how to automate your duties makes life easier and more productive. That’s one of the reason I’m working on getting all my scripts sanitized and up on github.

item.title: Linux USB IDs
item.description: Culled from a comment on Reddit. This is pretty handy. http://www.linux-usb.org/usb.ids
